channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: “Design, Innovation, and Society” alumni ASK US ANYTHING session!
item.description: Join a session with Design, Innovation, and Society alumni to discuss post-graduation life. Hear from recent and seasoned graduates about their careers and take this unique opportunity to ask them the questions you always wanted to ask about handling professional lives in the design worlds. 4/22 at 1pm in Sage 2211.
item.author: Students

item.title: Spring 2026 Student Course Evaluations Now Open
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 (all day) - Spring 2026 course evaluations are now open. The evaluation period will be open from April 14th through April 30th. Your feedback is very important to the Institute, the five schools, and their departments, so please complete evaluation surveys for all your courses! Evaluations will be available through Thursday, April 30, 2026.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week 4/19-4/26
item.description: Week of 4/19- 4/26: Last Round of Exams! Finish strong with one of our Success Center Review Sessions: -Physics I with SASE: Sunday, 4/19 (5-7pm) at the Success Center -Diff Eq.: Sunday, 4/19 (3-5pm) at the Success Center -Embedded Control: Monday 4/20 (7-9pm) at the Success Center -Cal II: Monday, 4/20 (7-9pm) in the Nason Classroom -Physics II: Tuesday, 4/21 (7-9pm) in Nason Classroom -Data Mgmt: Wednesday, 4/22 in ECAV (8-10pm) (Championship Classroom)
item.author: Students

item.title: 2026 Commencement Program Preferred Name
item.description: If you would like your preferred name (instead of your legal name) to appear in the 2026 Commencement program, please send an email to graduation@rpi.edu with your preference with the subject line “2026 Commencement Program Preferred Name”.To ensure your request can be accommodated, you must notify the Registrar’s Office no later than Thursday, April 23, 2026.If the Registrar does not receive a response by this deadline, they will use your legal name in the program.
item.author: Students

item.title: The Oath
item.description: April 23-25, 2026 Why do we choose to move in unison—to relinquish individuality to collectivity? What harmonies become possible in group movement, and what are its limits? Can difference and distinction persist within bodily sameness? Installation Annie-B Parson & Alla Kovgan | Big Dance Theater
item.author: Community

item.title: Yellow Lab Book Bar
item.description: April 23-25, 2026 Troy’s own Yellow Lab Vintage & Books hosts a pop-up bookstore and reading lounge filled with titles about space, place, performance, and embodiment, carefully selected by Yellow Lab’s Jessica DuPont along with selections by the festival’s artists and curator. Hang out on cozy couches with a wine or beer from Evelyn’s Café and peruse the shelves of new and used books for sale. Other books are just for display, offering glimpses into artists’ personal, annotated copies…
item.author: Community

item.title: Call for Fall 2026 NRB Beyond Blocks
item.description: Through Beyond Blocks, students participate in open houses, club fairs, workshops, and social events that help them stay connected, get involved, and thrive in their new environment. Submit an event: https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_ers5JWvVJ7QSmjQ Questions? Contact fye@rpi.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: Primordial
item.description: April 23-May 8, 2026 How are our bodies formed by the shifting ground that holds us up? How do we reshape and reform over time? How are we aligned in space and time with the environment around us? Installation Meg Foley & Carmichael Jones
item.author: Community

item.title: Community Supper
item.description: Thursday / April 23, 2026 at 6:00 pm The festival opens with a gathering: a free, family-style, long-table supper co-hosted by artist Yanira Castro, with creative contributions by partners and artists from across Troy, NY and the Capital Region, including food grounded in Indonesian ancestral traditions by Chef Ria Ibrahim and musical offerings by Taína Asili. Sharing food, stories of where it comes from, and rich conversations across the table about what it means to gather here and now—these…
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Libraries are pleased to provide Nexis+ AI
item.description: In addition to classic Nexis Uni, the RPI community now has access to Nexis+ AI, a strategic research solution designed to empower your academic endeavors by unlocking comprehensive insights and transforming raw data into targeted narratives. Seamlessly navigate vast collections of news, business, legal, financial, and company information using natural language search capabilities and intelligent citation-linked summaries. Whether building a thesis or drafting a research report, Nexis+ AI can…
item.author: Community

item.title: From Model-Optimal to Human-Optimal: Human-Centered Optimization for the Common Purposes of Life
item.description: Please join ISE on Wednesday, April 22 at 12:15 PM in LOW 3039 for a colloquium featuring Dr. Andrew Trapp’s research on From Model-Optimal to Human-Optimal: Human-Centered Optimization for the Common Purposes of Life! All are welcome!
item.author: Community

item.title: Earth Week Farmers Market April 23rd
item.description: Join Rensselaer Dining on Thursday, April 23rd for a Earth Week Farmers Market at the Student Union Patio from 11:00 am - 2:00 pm. Support local, eat fresh, and celebrate our planet together.
item.author: Community

item.title: Corpus Festival | April 23-25, 2026
item.description: A weekend of creative experiments and gatherings that attune us to how bodily forms and bodies in formation construct our world. How do we take part in society by physically showing up? We all play our part in shaping power, control, freedom, and mutual responsibility.
item.author: Community

item.title: Cognitive Science Colloquium with Dr. Fuat Balci on April 22nd
item.description: Please join the Cognitive Science Department on Wednesday, April 22nd, at 12:00 pm in Carnegie 113 for a presentation titled “The Stochastic Bridge: Diffusion in Timing and Time Awareness” by Dr. Fuat Balcı, Full Professor at the University of Manitoba and an internationally recognized expert in cognitive neuroscience.
item.author: Community

item.title: ISSS Hosts Free Immigration Workshop by NPZ Law Group
item.description: ISSS will host a virtual workshop presented by NPZ Group (Nachman, Phulwani, Zimovcak (NPZ) Law Group, P.C.). Hear from expert immigration lawyers about the visa options after graduation, including H-1B, O-1, and much more! Online event on Tuesday, April 21, 2026 from 2:00 p.m. - 3:30 p.m. Register at: https://tinyurl.com/NPZ-SP26-immigration
item.author: Community

item.title: Raft Installation
item.description: April 23-25, 2026 Can we hold our relationships to one another as sacred? How do we respond to our environment and effect change? What can we build together from a faltering world? Installation Yanira Castro | a canary torsi
item.author: Community

item.title: iEAR Presents! River of Grass Screening and Talk
item.description: Join us at The Sanctuary for Independent Media, 3361 6th Ave., Troy, NY, for a film screening of River of Grass, a documentary film which is described as “An ode to the Florida Everglades, told through the prescient writings of Marjory Stoneman Douglas, and those who today call the region home.” This film screening will be followed by a Q&A with filmmaker Sasha Wortzel who, ahead of the screening, will also offer a Be The Media! workshop called Community Collaboration and Reciprocity at 5pm.
item.author: Community

item.title: Teaching and Learning Collaboratory 2026 Spring In-Person RPI Faculty and Graduate TA’s Workshop: Teaching and Mentoring with Neurodivergence in Mind
item.description: In this session, faculty are introduced to a greater understanding of neurodivergence in college students. The program clarifies what neurodivergence is, what it isn’t, and offers practical tools for faculty that support structured, high-expectation learning environments where neurodivergent students can succeed. We’ll focus on strategies and concepts that support the maintenance of academic rigor while reducing unnecessary barriers to learning.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Fidelity Confidential Consultation (In Person)
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 23, 2026 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM - Schedule a confidential consultation to help you become better informed and more confident about your workplace retirement savings plan benefits. To schedule an appointment, call 1-800-642-7131, Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to midnight, or online at https://nb.fidelity.com/public/nbpreloginnav/rpi/microsite/#/tem/meet-with-us
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Staff: Leveraging Feedback and Career Opportunities
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 23, 2026 8:00 AM - 9:30 AM - As an employee, you work in partnership with your manager to align expectations and set meaningful goals. Throughout the year, you check in together, recalibrate as needed, and celebrate progress and achievements. This workshop focuses on supporting your individual career development, preparing for the annual manager evaluation, setting effective goals, and using tools that can help you advance your career.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Workshop: RPI MAX for Supervisors: Performance and Coaching
item.description:
Date: Thursday, April 23, 2026 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM - As a manager, you play a central role in guiding the performance process for your employees—working collaboratively to align expectations, support development, and monitor progress toward achieving goals. This workshop introduces the core principles of performance coaching, including how to identify and evaluate performance gaps, set effective goals, and lead the RPI MAX performance process. Participants will also explore practical tools for…

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
